prompt:
# ShiftWork Demo Simulation — Cloud Agent Instructions (Human Demoer Browser Drive)

You are a cloud agent simulating the human demoer for the ShiftWork doc-to-deploy pipeline. You drive the Opulent platform through the browser — login, upload, chat, watch, record video, evaluate, and report. You are NOT using API calls. Every action goes through the rendered UI.

## Context

ShiftWork hands a completed training doc to Opulent. Opulent's job is to find the doc, build a visual QA agent from it, run acceptance tests, and deliver the agent on the platform. You simulate the human demoer who logs in, uploads the doc, types the build instruction, and watches the whole thing unfold live. You record video of the entire session and give feedback on the demoer experience.

## Run details

- Run stamp: 20260701-080617
- Marker: SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-080617
- Completion marker: SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE
- Target: staging
- Timeout: 45 minutes
- Target site: https://shiftworkstudio.ai (fallback: https://example.com)
- Platform URL: ${OPULENT_PLATFORM_URL:-https://platform.opulentia.ai}

## Demo credentials

You need demo credentials to log in. Check these env vars:
- OPULENT_DEMO_EMAIL (or DEMO_EMAIL)
- OPULENT_DEMO_PASSWORD (or DEMO_PASSWORD)
- OPULENT_PLATFORM_URL (default: https://platform.opulentia.ai)

If credentials are not in env vars, ASK THE USER to provide them before starting. Do not proceed without credentials.

## Fixture

The training doc fixture is at:
/Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json

It contains:
- Business: ShiftWork Studio (Dan Cruden)
- Target URL: https://shiftworkstudio.ai
- Build target: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
- 8 build-critical answers (OP09, VQ01-VQ07) all owner_confirmed
- R01 (email connector) unconfirmed — draft-only email mode
- Gap review completed by shiftwork_crew
- Pipeline state: steps 5-11 completed_by_shiftwork, steps 12-14 not_started_opulent_owns

## Steps to execute

### Step 1: Load Ostack discipline
Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-engineering-discipline. This is mandatory. Also load the monitor skill pattern from .agent/skills/monitor/SKILL.md for video capture guidance.

### Step 2: Start video recording
Start ffmpeg screen capture of the browser viewport BEFORE navigating to the platform. Record at 30fps, H.264/AAC MP4, yuv420p, +faststart. Output to:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/demo-session-20260701-080617.mp4

If on macOS, also enable cua-driver trajectory recording:
  cua-driver recording start frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/trajectory-20260701-080617

The video must capture the full session from login to completion. See .agent/skills/cua-driver/RECORDING.md and .agent/skills/monitor/SKILL.md for the recording pattern.

If ffmpeg is not available, use cua-driver screenshot capture at every step as fallback.

### Step 3: Navigate to Opulent platform
Open a browser and navigate to the platform URL. Wait for the login page to fully load. Take a screenshot:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-03-login-page-20260701-080617.png

### Step 4: Log in with demo credentials
Enter the demo email and password into the login form. Click the login/submit button. Wait for the dashboard to load. Take a screenshot:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-04-dashboard-20260701-080617.png

If React #185 crash appears, record it as a MUST-PASS failure.

### Step 5: Create demo workspace
Create a new workspace named "ShiftWork Demo Simulation 20260701-080617" through the UI. Use the workspace creation button or menu. Wait for it to appear. Take a screenshot:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-05-workspace-20260701-080617.png

### Step 6: Upload training doc to workspace
Upload the training doc fixture to the workspace through the UI file upload feature. The file is at:
  /Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json

After upload, verify the file appears in the workspace file list. Take a screenshot:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-06-upload-20260701-080617.png

### Step 7: Open a new thread and type the build instruction
Open a new chat thread in the workspace. Type the full build instruction into the chat composer (the unified chat input). Take a screenshot of the typed prompt BEFORE sending:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-07a-prompt-typed-20260701-080617.png

Then press Enter or click Send. Take a screenshot AFTER sending:
  frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/screenshots/step-07b-prompt-sent-20260701-080617.png

The build instruction to type is:

---
You are a build agent in the Opulent platform. A ShiftWork training doc has been placed in your workspace. Your job is to find it, validate it, build a visual QA agent from it, create a cron schedule, and run acceptance tests.

STEP A: Find the training doc. Search the workspace for a file with schema marker "opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1" and leadId "SW-20260701-001". Use list_files and read_file.

STEP B: Validate the doc minimally. Check: (1) buildTarget is present and non-empty, (2) VQ07 answer exists, (3) VQ01 has targetUrl. If any missing, stop and report "cannot build: missing required fields".

STEP C: Run the automation modeler flow. Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-automation-modeler. Follow the intake flow and produce a Verified Automation Report. The automation: "Screenshot https://shiftworkstudio.ai at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 on a weekday schedule at 8 AM Central, compare against baseline, draft an email alert if a visual issue is found." Use the training doc answers as the spec. If the site is down, use https://example.com as fallback.

STEP D: Build the visual QA agent from the report. The agent: (1) screenshots the target URL at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844, (2) compares against baseline, (3) if issue found, drafts an alert email with subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT containing screenshots, URL, viewport, severity, issue description, fix suggestion, (4) stores screenshots and a run report in the workspace. Draft-only — do not send emails.

STEP E: Create the cron schedule. Call cron_manage action=create with cron="0 8 * * 1-5", timezone="America/Chicago", prompt describing the visual QA run, recurring=true. Record the schedule ID.

STEP F: Run the agent against 5 random pages on the target site. For each: screenshot at desktop and mobile, store in workspace, produce a report entry. All 5 must complete without crashing. If any crash, auto-fix up to 3 tries. After 3 failed fixes, stop and report.

STEP G: Run the acceptance test. Check: (1) at least 4 of 5 pages screenshotted successfully, (2) screenshots stored in workspace, (3) report produced. Must-pass: at least one screenshot + report produced. If 4/5 with must-pass OK, mark PASS. Otherwise FAIL.

STEP H: If acceptance passes, produce a how-to guide as markdown in the workspace. Title: "How to use your Visual QA Agent". Cover: login, see agent, read alerts, trigger manual run, understand schedule. 1-2 pages.

STEP I: Call autos_done with summary including: what was built, schedule ID, acceptance result, artifact locations, and marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-080617.
---

### Step 8: Watch the build agent work in real-time
Monitor the thread as the build agent works. Watch for:
- Streaming text responses appearing in the transcript
- Tool call chips appearing (skill_manage, list_files, read_file, document_create, cron_manage, write_file)
- Document panel showing the Verified Automation Report
- Screenshot artifacts appearing in workspace
- Any error messages or crashes

Take screenshots at each key moment:
  step-08a-build-started-20260701-080617.png
  step-08b-modeler-running-20260701-080617.png
  step-08c-automation-report-20260701-080617.png
  step-08d-cron-created-20260701-080617.png
  step-08e-screenshots-appearing-20260701-080617.png
  step-08f-acceptance-result-20260701-080617.png
  step-08g-how-to-guide-20260701-080617.png
  step-08h-autos-done-20260701-080617.png

Note any UX issues: slow rendering, missing tool chips, broken streaming, layout issues, React #185 crash. Wait up to 30 minutes for the build agent to complete (autos_done call).

### Step 9: Verify workspace artifacts through the UI
Navigate to the workspace file list. Verify: training doc, Verified Automation Report, screenshots (>=4), run report, how-to guide. Open each to confirm readable. Take a screenshot:
  step-09-workspace-artifacts-20260701-080617.png

### Step 10: Verify cron schedule through the UI
Navigate to the agent's schedule or trigger management UI. Verify the cron schedule (weekday 8 AM Central) is visible and active. Check manual on-demand trigger. Take a screenshot:
  step-10-cron-schedule-ui-20260701-080617.png

### Step 11: Evaluate against pass/fail criteria
Read the pass/fail criteria from:
  /Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-pass-fail-criteria.json

For each of the 26 criteria, mark PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, or SKIP with evidence (screenshots, video timestamps, transcript excerpts). Pay special attention to demoer-experience criteria: UI responsiveness, streaming quality, tool chip visibility, document panel rendering, crashes.

### Step 12: Write demoer feedback
Write a feedback section covering:
1. What worked well in the UI
2. What was confusing or broken
3. Any performance issues (slow rendering, streaming gaps)
4. Any crashes (React #185 or other)
5. Suggestions for improving the demo experience

This is qualitative feedback from the perspective of someone watching the demo live.

### Step 13: Produce findings report
Generate a findings report using the template at:
  /Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-template.json

Fill in all fields including: overall verdict, per-criterion results, workspace artifacts, demoer feedback, video evidence, screenshot evidence, errors, timing, recommendations. Write it to:
  /Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-20260701-080617.json

### Step 14: Stop video recording
Stop ffmpeg screen capture. Verify the video file exists and is playable:
  ffprobe -v error -show_streams frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/demo-session-20260701-080617.mp4

If cua-driver trajectory recording was enabled, stop it:
  cua-driver recording stop

### Step 15: Call autos_done with completion marker
Call autos_done with result including SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE and a summary of the findings, including the video path and overall verdict.

## Pass/Fail rules

- PASS: All 14 MUST-PASS criteria pass AND at least 21 of 26 total criteria pass
- PARTIAL: All 14 MUST-PASS criteria pass AND 16-20 of 26 pass
- FAIL: Any MUST-PASS fails OR fewer than 16 pass

## Must-pass criteria (14)

C01: Ostack discipline loaded
C02: Video recording started
C03: Platform login page loaded
C04: Logged in with demo credentials
C05: Demo workspace created through UI
C06: Training doc uploaded through UI
C07: Build instruction typed into chat composer
C08: Build agent streaming visible in transcript
C10: Automation modeler skill invocation visible
C11: Verified Automation Report visible in document panel
C13: Screenshots appearing in workspace during acceptance
C14: Run report visible in workspace
C15: autos_done visible in thread
C18: No React #185 crash during session
C22: Video recording valid and complete
C23: Findings report produced with verdict
C25: Completion marker emitted

## What to return

Return the findings report JSON. Include:
1. Overall verdict (PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL)
2. Per-criterion results with evidence and screenshot paths
3. Workspace artifact list (with visibleInUI flags)
4. Demoer feedback (5 areas)
5. Video file path and ffprobe validation
6. Screenshot paths
7. Errors encountered with video timestamps
8. Recommendations

## Key constraints

- Target site: https://shiftworkstudio.ai (fallback: https://example.com if down)
- Viewports: desktop 1280x900, mobile 390x844
- Cron: 0 8 * * 1-5 in America/Chicago (weekday 8 AM Central)
- Email: draft-only, do not send (R01 unconfirmed)
- No codebase access, no DOM access, screenshots only
- Daytona sandbox with secrets management
- Build agent: strong model (Opus or Sonnet). Running agent: cheap model (Haiku or open-source)
- No React #185 crash allowed (must-pass criterion C18)
- Acceptance: 5 random pages, 4/5 pass with must-pass (at least one screenshot + report)
- Video must capture the full session from login to completion

scenario.label: ShiftWork doc-to-deploy demo simulation — human demoer browser drive with video recording
scenario.description: A cloud agent simulates the human demoer: logs into the Opulent platform through the browser with demo credentials, uploads the training doc via the UI, types the build instruction into the chat composer, watches the full build run live (streaming transcript, tool calls, document panel, screenshots), records video of the entire session with ffmpeg, gives feedback on the demoer experience, and returns a findings report with PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL verdict.
scenario.notMode: This is NOT API orchestration. The cloud agent drives the browser UI exactly as a human demoer would. Every action goes through the rendered page — login form, file upload, chat composer, thread transcript, document panel, workspace files.

opulentOwns: Receive training doc from shared workspace
Build agent from training doc (automation modeler flow)
Acceptance test (5 random pages, screenshots, report)
Deliver agent on Opulent platform (cron + on-demand)
shiftworkOwns: Onboarding call (crew on Google Meet, not VAPI)

demoCredentials.note: The cloud agent uses demo credentials to log in as the human demoer. These are test credentials for the staging or production Opulent platform. The cloud agent should ask the user for credentials if not provided in env vars.
demoCredentials.fallback: If credentials are not in env vars, the cloud agent should ask the user to provide them before starting.

videoRecording.method: Screen capture of the browser viewport for the entire session
videoRecording.format: H.264/AAC MP4, yuv420p pixel format, +faststart moov atom
videoRecording.startBefore: Start recording BEFORE navigating to the platform. The video should capture the full session from login to completion.
videoRecording.stopAfter: Stop recording AFTER the findings report is produced and autos_done is called.
videoRecording.note: Follow the monitor skill pattern: capture the browser viewport, encode to web-safe H.264/AAC MP4. Use cua-driver recording for trajectory capture if on macOS. See .agent/skills/cua-driver/RECORDING.md and .agent/skills/monitor/SKILL.md.

moments: After training doc upload — file in workspace
After build instruction sent — message in thread
During build — automation modeler running (transcript visible)
During build — Verified Automation Report appears in document panel
During build — cron schedule created (tool call visible)
During acceptance — screenshots appearing in workspace

fixture.buildTarget: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
fixture.note: Fixture is a believable payload from ShiftWork after crew call + gap review. URL targets Dan's actual dummy app. Fallback URL is used if shiftworkstudio.ai is down.

instructionSet.summary: You are a cloud agent simulating the human demoer for the ShiftWork doc-to-deploy pipeline. You drive the Opulent platform through the browser — login, upload, chat, watch, record video, evaluate, and report. You are NOT using API calls. Every action goes through the rendered UI.

steps.name: Load Ostack discipline and monitor skill
steps.action: Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-engineering-discipline. This is mandatory before any work. Also load the monitor skill pattern for video capture guidance.
steps.passCriteria: skill_manage result includes 'opulentia-engineering-discipline' with status completed
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed without Ostack discipline.

steps.name: Start video recording
steps.action: Start ffmpeg screen capture of the browser viewport BEFORE navigating to the platform. Record at 30fps, H.264/AAC MP4, yuv420p, +faststart. Output to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/demo-session-20260701-080617.mp4. If on macOS, also enable cua-driver trajectory recording to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/trajectory-20260701-080617/. The video must capture the full session from login to completion.
steps.passCriteria: ffmpeg process is running and writing to the output file, or cua-driver recording state is enabled
steps.onFail: STOP. A demo without video evidence is a fail. If ffmpeg is not available, use cua-driver screenshot capture at every step as fallback.

steps.name: Navigate to Opulent platform
steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to the Opulent platform URL (from OPULENT_PLATFORM_URL env var, default https://platform.opulentia.ai). Wait for the login page to fully load. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation (agent-browser / Playwright / cua-driver launch_app with urls)
steps.expectedResult: Login page is visible and loaded
steps.passCriteria: Login page rendered with email and password fields visible
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed if the platform is unreachable.

steps.name: Log in with demo credentials
steps.action: Enter the demo email and password into the login form. Click the login/submit button. Wait for the dashboard to load. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — type into email field, type into password field, click submit
steps.passCriteria: Dashboard rendered with workspace list, agent list, or main UI visible. No error messages. No React #185 crash.
steps.onFail: If login fails, check credentials. If React #185 crash appears, record it as a MUST-PASS failure and continue if possible.

steps.name: Create demo workspace
steps.action: Create a new workspace named 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation 20260701-080617' through the UI. Use the workspace creation button or menu. Wait for the workspace to appear. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — click new workspace button, type workspace name, confirm
steps.expectedResult: Workspace created and visible in workspace list
steps.passCriteria: Workspace named 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation 20260701-080617' is visible
steps.onFail: If workspace creation fails through the UI, note the failure and attempt to continue with an existing workspace.

steps.name: Upload training doc to workspace
steps.action: Upload the training doc fixture to the workspace through the UI. Use the file upload feature in the workspace. The file is at frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json. After upload, verify the file appears in the workspace file list. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — use file upload UI element, select the fixture file, wait for upload confirmation
steps.expectedResult: Training doc file visible in workspace file list
steps.passCriteria: File named 'training-doc.post-call.json' appears in workspace files
steps.onFail: If file upload through the UI fails, note the failure. Try alternative upload methods (drag-and-drop, file picker). If all fail, record as a UI failure.

steps.name: Open a new thread and type the build instruction
steps.action: Open a new chat thread in the workspace. Type the full build instruction into the chat composer (the unified chat input). The build instruction is the same as the build prompt from the previous version — it tells the build agent to find the doc, validate it, run the automation modeler, build the visual QA agent, create cron, run 5 test pages, acceptance test, how-to guide, autos_done. Press Enter or click Send. Take a screenshot of the typed prompt before sending, and after sending.
steps.tool: browser automation — click new thread, type into chat composer, press Enter or click send
steps.buildPrompt:
You are a build agent in the Opulent platform. A ShiftWork training doc has been placed in your workspace. Your job is to find it, validate it, build a visual QA agent from it, create a cron schedule, and run acceptance tests.

STEP A: Find the training doc. Search the workspace for a file with schema marker 'opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1' and leadId 'SW-20260701-001'. Use list_files and read_file to find and read it.

STEP B: Validate the doc minimally. Check that: (1) buildTarget is present and non-empty, (2) acceptance test inputs are present (VQ07 answer exists), (3) targetUrl is present in VQ01. If any are missing, stop and report 'cannot build: missing required fields'.

STEP C: Run the full automation modeler flow. Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-automation-modeler. Follow the skill's intake flow: desired automation, connected apps, tools, memory, knowledge, workflow, dry-run evals, and produce a Verified Automation Report. The automation is: 'Screenshot https://shiftworkstudio.ai at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 on a weekday schedule at 8 AM Central, compare against baseline, draft an email alert if a visual issue is found.' Use the training doc answers as the spec. If the site is down, use the fallback URL 'https://example.com'.

STEP D: Build the visual QA agent from the Verified Automation Report. The agent should: (1) screenshot the target URL at desktop and mobile, (2) compare against baseline, (3) if issue found, draft an alert email with subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT containing screenshots, URL, viewport, severity, issue description, and fix suggestion, (4) store screenshots and a run report in the workspace. Draft-only mode — do not send emails. R01 email connector is unconfirmed.

STEP E: Create the cron schedule. Call cron_manage action=create with cron='0 8 * * 1-5', timezone='America/Chicago', prompt describing the visual QA run, recurring=true. Record the schedule ID. Also note that manual on-demand trigger should be available.

STEP F: Run the agent against 5 random pages on shiftworkstudio.ai. For each page: screenshot at desktop and mobile, store screenshots in workspace, produce a report entry. If the site is down, use the fallback URL 'https://example.com'. All 5 must complete without crashing. If any crash, attempt auto-fix (up to 3 tries). After 3 failed fixes, stop and report the error.

STEP G: Run the acceptance test. Check that: (1) at least 4 of 5 pages were screenshotted successfully, (2) screenshots are stored in the workspace, (3) a report was produced. The must-pass case is: at least one screenshot + report produced. If 4/5 pass with must-pass OK, mark acceptance as PASS. Otherwise mark as FAIL.

STEP H: If acceptance passes, produce a how-to guide as a markdown document in the workspace. Title: 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'. Cover: how to log in, see your agent, read alerts, trigger a manual run, understand the schedule. Keep it to 1-2 pages.

STEP I: Call autos_done with a summary of what was built, the schedule ID, the acceptance test result, and the workspace artifact locations. Include the marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-080617 in the autos_done summary.
steps.expectedResult: Build instruction message appears in the thread. Build agent starts processing.
steps.passCriteria: Message is visible in the thread and the agent begins responding (streaming text or tool calls appear)
steps.onFail: If the chat composer does not accept the message, record the UI failure. Try alternative input methods.

steps.name: Watch the build agent work in real-time
steps.action: Monitor the thread as the build agent works. Watch for: (1) streaming text responses, (2) tool call chips appearing (skill_manage, list_files, read_file, document_create, cron_manage, write_file), (3) document panel showing the Verified Automation Report, (4) screenshot artifacts appearing in workspace, (5) any error messages or crashes. Take screenshots at each key moment. Note any UX issues: slow rendering, missing tool chips, broken streaming, layout issues, React #185 crash. Wait up to 30 minutes for the build agent to complete (autos_done call).
steps.tool: browser automation — periodic screenshots, read thread content, check for errors
steps.expectedResult: Build agent completes all steps A-I and calls autos_done with the marker
steps.passCriteria: autos_done visible in thread with marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-080617
steps.onFail: If agent does not complete within 30 minutes, record what steps were visible and what failed. Note any UI issues observed during the wait.

steps.name: Verify workspace artifacts through the UI
steps.action: Navigate to the workspace file list in the UI. Verify that: (1) training doc is present, (2) Verified Automation Report is present, (3) screenshots are present (at least 4), (4) run report is present, (5) how-to guide is present. Open each artifact to confirm it is readable. Take screenshots of the file list and at least one opened artifact.
steps.tool: browser automation — navigate workspace files, open documents
steps.expectedResult: All artifacts visible and readable through the UI
steps.passCriteria: At least: training doc, Verified Automation Report, screenshots (>=4), run report, how-to guide are visible in the workspace UI
steps.onFail: Record which artifacts are missing or unreadable through the UI.

steps.name: Verify cron schedule through the UI
steps.action: Navigate to the agent's schedule settings or trigger management UI. Verify that the cron schedule (weekday 8 AM Central) is visible and active. Check that manual on-demand trigger is available. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — navigate to schedule/trigger settings
steps.expectedResult: Cron schedule visible with correct expression and timezone. On-demand trigger available.
steps.passCriteria: Schedule shows '0 8 * * 1-5' or 'Weekday 8:00 AM' in America/Chicago. Manual trigger button or option is visible.
steps.onFail: If schedule is not visible through the UI, note the failure. The schedule may exist in the backend but not be surfaced in the UI.

steps.name: Evaluate against pass/fail criteria
steps.action: Compare the observed behavior and artifacts against all criteria in demo-pass-fail-criteria.json. For each criterion, mark PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, or SKIP with evidence (screenshots, video timestamps, transcript excerpts). Pay special attention to demoer-experience criteria: was the UI responsive, did streaming work, were tool chips visible, did the document panel render, were there any crashes.
steps.passCriteria: All criteria scored with evidence from the browser session
steps.onFail: Record which criteria could not be evaluated and why.

steps.name: Write feedback and recommendations
steps.action: Write a feedback section for the demoer experience. Cover: (1) What worked well in the UI, (2) What was confusing or broken, (3) Any performance issues (slow rendering, streaming gaps), (4) Any crashes (React #185 or other), (5) Suggestions for improving the demo experience. This is qualitative feedback from the perspective of someone watching the demo live.
steps.expectedResult: Feedback section written with actionable observations
steps.passCriteria: Feedback covers all 5 areas with specific examples from the session

steps.name: Produce findings report
steps.action: Generate a findings report in JSON format using the demo-findings-template.json structure. Include: scenario ID, run stamp, overall verdict (PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL), per-criterion results with evidence, workspace artifact list, video file path, screenshot paths, demoer feedback, errors encountered, and recommendations. Write it to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-20260701-080617.json.
steps.passCriteria: Findings report is valid JSON with all fields populated including video path and feedback
steps.onFail: Record the failure to produce the report itself as a finding.

steps.name: Stop video recording
steps.action: Stop the ffmpeg screen capture. Verify the video file exists and is playable (ffprobe check). If cua-driver trajectory recording was enabled, stop it too. Verify the trajectory directory has turn folders.
steps.tool: ffmpeg (kill process) or cua-driver set_recording enabled=false
steps.expectedResult: Video file exists, is valid MP4, and is playable
steps.passCriteria: ffprobe confirms valid H.264/AAC MP4 with yuv420p and +faststart. File size > 0. Duration > 60 seconds.
steps.onFail: If video is corrupt or missing, note it as a critical failure. Screenshots may serve as fallback evidence.

steps.name: Call autos_done with completion marker
steps.action: Call autos_done with the completion marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE and a summary of the findings, including the video path and overall verdict.
steps.onFail: Record failure to call autos_done as a finding.

verificationSignals.description: Build instruction typed and sent through chat composer
Build agent started automation modeler flow (visible in transcript)
Verified Automation Report visible in document panel
Cron schedule creation visible in transcript (tool chip)
Screenshots appearing in workspace during acceptance test
Build agent completed with autos_done (visible in transcript)

runbookOverrides.scopeDecider: Auto-decide from doc (Runbook says human decides — overridden for POC)
runbookOverrides.steps5to11Ownership: ShiftWork (Runbook says Opulent — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.vapi: Not handled by Opulent (Runbook says Opulent or VAPI — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.customerLogin: Skip for POC (Runbook requires login — deferred)
runbookOverrides.runtimePayerEnforcement: Skip for POC (Runbook requires enforcement — deferred)
runbookOverrides.dreamsInMemory: Skip for POC (promised in meeting — deferred)

runbookFollows.deliveryOwner: Opulent owns delivery, ShiftWork schedules + pays (followed)
runbookFollows.roi: Opulent exposes logs, ShiftWork does check-in (followed)

modelStrategy.buildAgent: Strong model (Opus or Sonnet) for automation modeler flow
modelStrategy.runningAgent: Cheap model (Haiku or open-source) for visual QA execution
modelStrategy.rationale: Tiered strategy from June 12 meeting. Coordinator on strong model, execution on cheap model.

sandbox.rationale: Promised to Braden in June 12 meeting. Granular access controls, VM sandboxing, secrets management.

emailMode.reason: R01 email connector unconfirmed in training doc. Owner requested draft-only. Live send is post-build wiring.
emailMode.alertContents: Subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT, screenshots, page URL, viewport, severity, issue description, fix suggestion
emailMode.note: Draft-only mode auto-enforces the do-not-email-customers rule because nothing is sent.

targetSite.rationale: Dan's actual dummy app from the June 12 meeting. Fallback if site is down during demo.

acceptanceTest.inputs: 5 random pages on the target site
acceptanceTest.passCriteria: 4/5 pages screenshotted successfully with report produced
acceptanceTest.mustPass: At least one screenshot + report produced
acceptanceTest.fixLoop: Up to 2 rounds, each capped at 2 business days. After 2 fails, human decides: rebuild narrower, partial with credit, or refund.
acceptanceTest.approver: Named human reviews the auto-run score
acceptanceTest.note: 5 easy-to-hit pre-validated test criteria. No seeded defects.

twoAgentsRequired.pocSuccessMeasure: Agents deployed for both Dan Cruden and Braden Silcott
twoAgentsRequired.buildOrder: Sequential. Dan first (shiftworkstudio.ai), Braden second (after intake received).

criteria.description: Pass/fail rubric for the ShiftWork doc-to-deploy demo simulation — human demoer browser drive with video recording. Each criterion is scored PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL with evidence. Overall verdict is PASS only if all MUST-PASS criteria pass and at least 80% of all criteria pass.

verdictRules.pass: All MUST-PASS criteria pass AND at least 80% of all criteria pass
verdictRules.partial: All MUST-PASS criteria pass AND 60-79% of all criteria pass
verdictRules.fail: Any MUST-PASS criterion fails OR fewer than 60% of all criteria pass

criteria.name: Ostack discipline loaded
criteria.check: skill_manage was called for opulentia-engineering-discipline and completed successfully
criteria.evidenceRequired: Tool call log showing skill_manage with opulentia-engineering-discipline, status completed
criteria.passCondition: skill_manage result includes 'opulentia-engineering-discipline' with status completed
criteria.failCondition: skill_manage was not called, or failed, or wrong skill was loaded

criteria.name: Video recording started
criteria.check: ffmpeg screen capture or cua-driver recording was started before navigating to the platform
criteria.evidenceRequired: ffmpeg process running or cua-driver recording state enabled, confirmed before step 3
criteria.passCondition: Recording was started before the browser navigated to the platform
criteria.failCondition: Recording was not started or started after navigation

criteria.name: Platform login page loaded
criteria.check: The Opulent platform login page loaded in the browser with email and password fields visible
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of login page with form fields visible
criteria.passCondition: Login page rendered with email and password fields
criteria.failCondition: Page did not load or login form is not visible

criteria.name: Logged in with demo credentials
criteria.check: Demo credentials were entered and login succeeded. Dashboard is visible.
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of dashboard after login, no error messages
criteria.passCondition: Dashboard rendered with workspace list, agent list, or main UI visible. No error messages.
criteria.failCondition: Login failed, error message shown, or dashboard did not load

criteria.name: Demo workspace created through UI
criteria.check: A workspace was created through the UI with the name 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation {{RUN_STAMP}}'
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing the workspace in the workspace list
criteria.passCondition: Workspace is visible in the UI with the correct name
criteria.failCondition: Workspace was not created or is not visible in the UI

criteria.name: Training doc uploaded through UI
criteria.check: The training doc fixture was uploaded to the workspace through the UI file upload feature
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing the file in the workspace file list
criteria.passCondition: Training doc file is visible in the workspace file list in the UI
criteria.failCondition: File upload failed or file is not visible in the UI

criteria.name: Build instruction typed into chat composer
criteria.check: The full build instruction was typed into the unified chat input and sent to a new thread
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the typed message in the composer before sending, and screenshot of the sent message in the thread
criteria.passCondition: Message is visible in the thread and the build agent begins responding
criteria.failCondition: Chat composer did not accept the message or the message was not sent

criteria.name: Build agent streaming visible in transcript
criteria.check: The build agent's streaming text responses are visible in the thread transcript as they are generated
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing streaming text or agent response in the thread
criteria.passCondition: Agent responses are visible and updating in real-time in the thread UI
criteria.failCondition: No agent response visible or streaming is broken/stalled

criteria.name: Tool call chips visible in transcript
criteria.check: Tool call chips (skill_manage, list_files, read_file, document_create, cron_manage, write_file) are visible in the transcript as the build agent works
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing tool call chips in the thread
criteria.passCondition: At least 3 different tool call chips are visible in the transcript
criteria.failCondition: No tool call chips visible

criteria.name: Automation modeler skill invocation visible
criteria.check: The skill_manage call for opulentia-automation-modeler is visible in the transcript
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing skill_manage tool chip or text mentioning opulentia-automation-modeler in the thread
criteria.passCondition: Automation modeler invocation is visible in the transcript
criteria.failCondition: No evidence of automation modeler in the transcript

criteria.name: Verified Automation Report visible in document panel
criteria.check: The Verified Automation Report appears in the document panel or workspace as the build agent produces it
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing the report in the document panel or workspace file list
criteria.passCondition: Report is visible in the UI with title containing 'Verified Automation Report'
criteria.failCondition: Report was not produced or not visible in the UI

criteria.name: Cron schedule creation visible in transcript
criteria.check: The cron_manage tool call is visible in the transcript with the schedule details
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing cron_manage tool chip or schedule creation text in the thread
criteria.passCondition: Cron schedule creation is visible in the transcript
criteria.failCondition: No evidence of cron schedule creation in the transcript

criteria.name: Screenshots appearing in workspace during acceptance
criteria.check: Screenshot artifacts appear in the workspace as the build agent runs the acceptance test
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the workspace file list showing screenshot files, or screenshot of the workspace with new image artifacts
criteria.passCondition: At least 4 screenshot files are visible in the workspace
criteria.failCondition: No screenshots visible in the workspace

criteria.name: Run report visible in workspace
criteria.check: A run report is visible in the workspace after the acceptance test
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the workspace file list showing the report file, or screenshot of the report opened in the UI
criteria.passCondition: Report file is visible in the workspace UI
criteria.failCondition: No report visible in the workspace

criteria.name: autos_done visible in thread
criteria.check: The build agent's autos_done call is visible in the thread transcript with the demo marker
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the thread showing autos_done completion with marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_{{RUN_STAMP}}
criteria.passCondition: autos_done is visible in the thread with the marker
criteria.failCondition: autos_done is not visible or marker is missing

criteria.name: How-to guide visible in workspace
criteria.check: The how-to guide is visible in the workspace after acceptance passes
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the workspace file list showing the how-to guide, or screenshot of the guide opened in the UI
criteria.passCondition: How-to guide is visible in the workspace UI with title containing 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'
criteria.failCondition: No how-to guide visible in the workspace

criteria.name: Cron schedule visible in UI settings
criteria.check: The cron schedule (weekday 8 AM Central) is visible in the agent's schedule or trigger management UI
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the schedule settings showing the cron expression or human-readable schedule
criteria.passCondition: Schedule shows '0 8 * * 1-5' or 'Weekday 8:00 AM' in America/Chicago timezone in the UI
criteria.failCondition: Schedule is not visible in the UI
criteria.partialCondition: Schedule exists but timezone or expression is wrong in the UI

criteria.name: No React #185 crash during session
criteria.check: No React error #185 (Maximum update depth exceeded) occurred at any point during the browser session
criteria.evidenceRequired: No 'Minified React error #185' or 'Maximum update depth exceeded' visible in any screenshot, page text, or browser console during the entire session
criteria.passCondition: No React #185 crash occurred during the entire session
criteria.failCondition: React #185 crash occurred at any point

criteria.name: Streaming responsive (no long stalls)
criteria.check: The build agent's streaming responses did not stall for more than 30 seconds at any point during the session
criteria.evidenceRequired: Video evidence showing continuous streaming activity, or timestamps showing no gaps > 30s in agent responses
criteria.passCondition: No streaming stalls longer than 30 seconds
criteria.failCondition: Streaming stalled for more than 60 seconds at any point

criteria.name: Document panel renders correctly
criteria.check: The document panel renders the Verified Automation Report and how-to guide correctly — readable, no layout breakage, no missing content
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the document panel showing rendered content
criteria.passCondition: Document panel renders content correctly with no layout issues
criteria.failCondition: Document panel is broken, empty, or has layout issues
criteria.partialCondition: Document panel renders but with minor visual issues

criteria.name: No error messages visible to demoer
criteria.check: No unhandled error messages, toast notifications, or error boundaries were visible to the demoer during the session
criteria.evidenceRequired: No error toasts, error banners, or error pages in any screenshot or video frame
criteria.passCondition: No error messages visible during the session
criteria.failCondition: Error messages were visible to the demoer
criteria.partialCondition: Minor warnings visible but no critical errors

criteria.name: Video recording valid and complete
criteria.check: The video recording is a valid MP4 file, playable, with duration > 60 seconds, capturing the session from login to completion
criteria.evidenceRequired: ffprobe output confirming valid H.264/AAC MP4 with yuv420p and +faststart, file size > 0, duration > 60s
criteria.passCondition: Video is valid, playable, duration > 60s, captures login through completion
criteria.failCondition: Video is missing, corrupt, or shorter than 60 seconds
criteria.partialCondition: Video is valid but does not capture the full session (started late or stopped early)

criteria.name: Findings report produced with verdict
criteria.check: A findings report JSON was produced with overall verdict, per-criterion results, evidence, video path, and demoer feedback
criteria.evidenceRequired: Findings report JSON file at the expected path with all fields populated
criteria.passCondition: Findings report is valid JSON with overall verdict, all criteria scored, evidence, video path, and feedback section
criteria.failCondition: No findings report or it is missing required fields

criteria.name: Demoer feedback written
criteria.check: A feedback section was written covering: what worked well, what was confusing or broken, performance issues, crashes, and suggestions for improvement
criteria.evidenceRequired: Feedback section in the findings report with all 5 areas covered
criteria.passCondition: Feedback covers all 5 areas with specific examples from the session
criteria.failCondition: No feedback section or it is missing required areas
criteria.partialCondition: Feedback covers some but not all 5 areas

criteria.name: Completion marker emitted
criteria.check: The demo simulation completed with the SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE marker
criteria.evidenceRequired: autos_done call with result including SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE
criteria.passCondition: Completion marker was emitted
criteria.failCondition: Completion marker was not emitted

criteria.name: Fallback URL used if primary is down
criteria.check: If shiftworkstudio.ai was unreachable during the acceptance test, the build agent used the fallback URL https://example.com
criteria.evidenceRequired: Video or transcript evidence showing fallback URL usage if primary was down, or primary was reachable so fallback was not needed
criteria.passCondition: Either primary URL worked, or fallback was used when primary was down
criteria.failCondition: Primary was down and no fallback was used
criteria.skipCondition: Primary URL was reachable (fallback not needed — mark as PASS with note)

summary.passThreshold: All 14 MUST-PASS pass AND at least 80% of all 26 pass (>= 21)
summary.failThreshold: Any MUST-PASS fails OR fewer than 16 criteria pass

callInfo.callRoutingNote: Crew call on Google Meet. VAPI is not handled by Opulent. ShiftWork owns the call and recording.

buildTarget.target: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
buildTarget.ownerStatement: I want an agent to screenshot shiftworkstudio.ai on desktop and mobile and email me when something looks broken.
buildTarget.provisionalTargetFromResearch: Ops admin automation for invoice and inbox triage
buildTarget.changeReason: Owner's top felt problem is catching landing page breakage before customers notice, not invoice chasing. Research brief flagged this as likely core problem. Owner confirmed it out loud.

answers.question: How do invoices go out today, and how do you chase a late payment?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely QuickBooks or paper invoices based on industry norms. Chase process unknown.
answers.ownerAnswer: I send invoices through QuickBooks after the deposit hits. If someone is late I send an email at 15 days, a text at 30 days, and I call at 45 days. Honestly I hate chasing people.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How much money sits unpaid past due in a normal month?
answers.draftAnswer: Unknown. Research could not find public data on accounts receivable.
answers.ownerAnswer: Probably 8 to 12 thousand in a normal month. It is usually one or two jobs where the final invoice is sitting unpaid.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What lands in your inbox that you wish you didn't have to sort?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely spam, vendor emails, and lead form confirmations based on industry patterns.
answers.ownerAnswer: Vendor emails, spam, Google review notifications, and lead form confirmations from the website. The lead confirmations matter but I do not need to read them one by one.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: Which emails actually need you, and which could be filed or handled for you?
answers.ownerAnswer: Customer complaints, estimate requests, and anything from my crew leads needs me. Everything else could be filed or summarized. Vendor emails can be auto-filed.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How do you schedule your team and your work today?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely a mix of text messages and a shared calendar based on industry norms.
answers.ownerAnswer: I use a Google Calendar for my own schedule and a group text for the crew. It breaks down when two jobs overlap or someone calls out sick.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What goes wrong with scheduling in a normal week?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely double-books, last-minute changes, and no-shows based on industry patterns.
answers.ownerAnswer: Double-books when I forget to update the calendar after a text conversation. Last-minute reschedules from customers. Crew no-shows maybe once a month.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How do you hire today, and where does it bog down?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely Indeed and referrals based on industry norms.
answers.ownerAnswer: Word of mouth and referrals mostly. Sometimes Indeed. It bogs down at screening because I do not have time to call everyone back.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What makes someone a good fit, and what's a quick no?
answers.ownerAnswer: Good fit is someone who shows up on time, has done residential work before, and does not complain about the drive. Quick no is anyone who no-shows the first interview or lies about experience.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What admin task eats the most of your time each week?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely checking whether the landing page still looks right after edits, based on the build request.
answers.ownerAnswer: Checking whether the landing page still looks right after edits. I usually notice problems only after a customer says the quote button is missing or a photo is broken.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed on call. This is the top felt problem that drives the first build.

answers.question: What page should the visual quality agent monitor first?
answers.draftAnswer: Monitor https://shiftworkstudio.ai first based on the business domain.
answers.ownerAnswer: Monitor https://shiftworkstudio.ai first. The highest priority route is the home landing page at / because that is where paid traffic lands.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed URL and priority route on call.

answers.question: Which viewports matter?
answers.draftAnswer: Desktop and mobile based on industry norms. Exact dimensions unknown.
answers.ownerAnswer: Check desktop at 1280 by 900 and mobile at 390 by 844. Mobile matters most because most leads come from phones.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed exact viewport dimensions on call.

answers.question: What counts as a visual inconsistency?
answers.ownerAnswer: Email me if text overlaps, the hero image is missing, the Get a Quote button is hidden or unreachable, there is horizontal scroll on mobile, a section is blank, navigation covers content, the form is cut off, or the page has console errors.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner provided the full inconsistency criteria list on call.

answers.question: What should the email alert include?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely a screenshot and a description of the problem.
answers.ownerAnswer: Send screenshots, the page URL, viewport size, what changed, how severe it is, and a short plain-English fix suggestion. Put VISUAL QA ALERT in the subject.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed alert contents and subject prefix on call.

answers.question: Who should receive alerts?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely the owner email based on the lead record.
answers.ownerAnswer: Send alerts to dan@shiftworkstudio.ai. Do not email customers.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed recipient and the do-not-email-customers rule on call.

answers.question: How often should the agent run?
answers.ownerAnswer: Run every weekday at 8:00 AM Central and also on demand when I ask for a check. For the first build, manual on-demand is acceptable if scheduling is not wired yet.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed schedule and manual fallback on call.

answers.question: What should the first successful run prove?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely that screenshots are captured and an alert is produced when an issue is found.
answers.ownerAnswer: It should capture desktop and mobile screenshots of the landing page, compare them to the baseline, find no critical issue or describe the issue, and produce the exact alert email draft if anything is wrong.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed acceptance test on call.

answers.question: Which email connector should the agent use?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely Outlook or Gmail based on the owner email.
answers.ownerAnswer: I use Gmail for my business email. But I am not sure about giving an agent access to it. Can it just draft the email and I send it?
answers.gapReviewNote: RESEARCH GAP. Owner mentioned Gmail but did not confirm connector access. Owner requested draft-only mode. This stays unconfirmed. Live email send requires explicit approval and connector setup.

gapReview.signOffNote: All 8 build-critical questions are owner-confirmed. R01 (email connector) remains unconfirmed. Owner requested draft-only email mode. Build can proceed with draft-only email. Live send is a post-build wiring step.

storageLocation.note: Training doc lives in the shared Opulent workspace. Build agent finds it by schema marker + leadId. Recording is ShiftWork-owned, not read by the build agent.

intakeAnswerReference.relationship: The intake answer is the pre-call artifact. This training doc is the post-call artifact. The intake answer carries draft answers tagged draft_from_research_unconfirmed. This training doc carries the same answers updated to owner_confirmed or corrected_by_owner, plus the confirmed build target, plus the gap review sign-off.

criteriaResults.name: Ostack discipline loaded
criteriaResults.evidence: Tool call log or transcript excerpt

artifacts.type: training_doc | verified_automation_report | screenshot | run_report | how_to_guide | findings_report

demoerExperience.whatWorkedWell: What looked good during the demo from the demoer's perspective
demoerExperience.whatWasConfusingOrBroken: What was confusing, broken, or unexpected in the UI
demoerExperience.performanceIssues: Any slow rendering, streaming gaps, or latency issues observed
demoerExperience.crashesObserved: Any crashes (React #185 or other) observed during the session
demoerExperience.suggestionsForImprovement: Actionable suggestions to improve the demo experience

react185Status.evidence: No React error #185 found in any screenshot or video frame

rawData.toolCallLog: Path to full tool call log if captured
rawData.agentTranscript: Path to full agent transcript if captured
rawData.browserConsoleLog: Path to browser console log if captured
